steps.title: Spot the contrast between prior belief and new findings
steps.content: The passage says researchers had thought defensive ultrasound was exclusive to tiger moths, hawkmoths, and one species of geometer moths, but then found that 52 genera—including several from the geometer family—produced such clicks when exposed to bat echolocation.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
The correct answer claims that anti‑bat ultrasound production is a more prevalent defense strategy among large‑bodied moths than previously known.

The other three either introduce unrelated ideas, suggest limited prevalence, or shift focus away from the surprise of wider occurrence.

That is the correct answer.
